Fixed and independently re-verified in .gitea/workflows/services-deploy.yml: GITHUB_OUTPUT replaces ::set-output, check-changes now diffs the full github.event.before..after push range (HEAD~1 fallback only for first-push all-zero SHA), setup-ansible.sh renamed in both remaining grep patterns, !services/matterbridge/** exclusion added. Uncommitted in working tree pending user review/commit.

Fixed and independently re-verified in infrastructure/ups-monitoring/main.tf: both occurrences of ../../../services/nut/main.yml corrected to ../../services/nut/main.yml. tofu validate passes cleanly. Uncommitted in working tree pending user review/commit.

Live wakapi LXC (192.168.7.6) drift assessment complete: version, config, systemd unit, and password_salt all match the repo exactly (changed=0 via ansible --check --diff after working around the test-playbook hosts: gap). tofu plan shows only a non-destructive mountpoint backup flag drift (exit 2, 0 add/1 change/0 destroy) -- no LXC destroy/replace risk. Verdict: SAFE TO CONVERGE. Follow-ups filed for the 3 residual findings (0xn.1/.2/.3).

Live n8n container audited on beelink-gti: version 2.9.4 (image label + docker exec confirmed), the ./dev build context genuinely exists live at /opt/n8n-ai-starter-kit/dev/Dockerfile (thin wrapper FROM n8nio/n8n:latest patching @langchain/ollama for Ollama integration), and the live rendered compose file matches the repo's .j2 template exactly. VERDICT for w97: commit ./dev into services/nvidia-gpu/dev/ rather than pinning a version -- the Dockerfile builds FROM a moving upstream tag and n8n_version is unreferenced. No secrets were ever printed to transcript (names/presence only for sensitive vars).

Root cause confirmed (Calico IPAM exhaustion caused by a ~1280-IP leak burst on 2026-05-27 going unreclaimed because calico-kube-controllers was itself deadlocked) and fixed live via calicoctl ipam check + release, approved by user. calico-kube-controllers is now Running for the first time in its recorded history; cluster-wide pod status recovered from near-total ContainerCreating to 30/32 Running. Two small residuals (fluent-bit CrashLoopBackOff, an orphaned nats-debug pod) tracked separately, not part of this incident.

User confirmed intentional: nightly 23:30 shutdown is paired with a BIOS RTC wake timer that powers the host back on automatically (matches observed ~02:30 daily boot pattern). Documented as intentionally unmanaged, not a bug.

False alarm from checking too early -- the queued job completed successfully in ~11 minutes (ack_pending went 1->0, output stream gained a message, zero redeliveries). scraper-mq's historical DNS-failure crash loop was real but is resolved as a side effect of homelab-infra-1jj. Both n8n flows confirmed working.

Adopted into services/fission role (fission_keda_enabled, chart pinned to keda-2.17.2 matching the live install). Converged live against beelink-gti: helm repo added, KEDA release came back 'ok' (unchanged) confirming exact match to the pre-existing manual install. No live disruption.

New services/tailscale role added (plain client, --accept-dns=false, reuses the existing dnsdist reusable authkey), wired into host_roles/infrastructure.yml. Converged live against beelink-gti: tailscale authenticated successfully and 'tailscale status' now shows it online on the tailnet (previously stuck logged-out). Also fixed a real apt source conflict this surfaced: the manual install's legacy /etc/apt/sources.list.d/tailscale.list + /usr/share/keyrings/tailscale-archive-keyring.gpg collided with the ansible role's deb822-style source, breaking apt-get update entirely; removed the superseded legacy pair (byte-identical GPG key, confirmed via sha256sum) so ansible is now the sole source of truth.

Adopted into services/microk8s role (microk8s_fluentbit_enabled toggle) with namespace/ServiceAccount/ClusterRole/ClusterRoleBinding/ConfigMap/DaemonSet transcribed verbatim from the live objects -- converged live against beelink-gti with zero diff (byte-identical to what was already running). Root cause of the CrashLoopBackOff (312,288 stale per-restart log files in /var/log/containers, 86GB in /var/log/pods, exceeding fluent-bit's open-file capacity) cleaned up live: removed files older than 7 days plus their /var/log/pods targets, then pruned emptied pod directories. Pod is now 1/1 Running (was CrashLoopBackOff with 93986 restarts). Discovered a separate, pre-existing issue in the process -- Graylog itself is unreachable on both GELF and syslog ports -- filed as homelab-infra-51a.2.1, not part of this bead's scope.

Fully resolved. (1) AVX fix: VM 101 (ubuntu-22) CPU model changed from x86-64-v2-AES to x86-64-v3,flags=+aes on its Proxmox host (brices-imac, 192.168.7.91, part of the 3-node 'homelab' cluster with proxmox-main and imac-pro-pve); confirmed avx/avx2 present after a full VM stop/start. (2) Mongo data corruption: backed up the graylog-mongodb volume (208MB tarball on ubuntu-22 at /root/graylog-mongo-backup/), then ran mongod --repair -- first attempt still hit the same WT_PANIC during mandatory journal replay, so moved the WiredTiger journal aside (kept, not deleted, at journal.corrupt-backup) to force recovery from the last checkpoint instead, then repair completed cleanly (exit 0). (3) Brought the full compose stack up: mongodb/datanode/graylog all stable, Graylog's HTTP API healthy, confirmed beelink-gti can now reach graylog-direct.cusack-ruth.name on both 12201 (GELF) and 5140 (syslog as actually exposed by the compose file). fluent-bit on beelink-gti is now successfully flushing chunks to Graylog (previously 100% connection-refused). Residual, separate finding not chased further: microk8s_syslog_server's default hostname 'graylog.cusack-ruth.name' (used by the OTHER host-level rsyslog-forwarding feature, port 514) resolves to a completely different host (beelink, 192.168.7.198) than where Graylog actually runs (ubuntu-22) -- looks like a stale DNS entry from before Graylog moved hosts, worth a follow-up look if host-level syslog forwarding is ever audited.

Both incidents fully resolved as described. open-webui running on pinned dev digest, confirmed serving (GET /ready 200). gitea running on ghcr.io/go-gitea/gitea:1.26.1, confirmed HTTP 200 at https://gitea.cusack-ruth.name/. No data loss; backups retained at /opt/open-webui/data/webui.db.pre-incident-backup-* and /volume1/docker/gitea-incident-backup/.
